issuesFound.explanation: The `setExpanded` utility function is defined to manage `aria-expanded`, but the `toggle` function in the `mobileNav` module (which controls the primary navigation drawer) does not call it or update the attribute directly. Screen reader users will not be notified whether the navigation menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1145: `function toggle() { _open ? close() : open(); setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, _open); }`

issuesFound.title: toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `dismiss` function in the `toastManager` removes toast notifications from the DOM but does not update the state of the trigger. More critically, the `show` function creates a dismiss button that lacks a toggle state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1015: `on(dismissBtn, EVENTS.CLICK, () => { setAttr(dismissBtn, ARIA.PRESSED, 'true'); dismiss(toast); });`

issuesFound.title: dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `_activate` function in the `createScrollSpy` module updates the `aria-current` attribute and CSS classes on navigation links as the user scrolls, but it does not announce the new active section to screen readers. Users relying on assistive technology will not know their location relative to page sections during scroll.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1685: `if (link) { link.setAttribute(cfg.activeAttr, cfg.activeValue); addClass(link, cfg.activeClass); liveRegion.announce('Navigated to ' + section.id); }`

issuesFound.title: form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `_renderResult` function in the `inlineValidator` module manages error message visibility and `aria-describedby` associations, but it fails to set the `aria-invalid` attribute on the input element itself. The contrast ratio is approximately 2.3:1, which is below the 4.5:1 minimum for normal text and 3:1 for large text (18px), affecting users with low vision or colour blindness.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not informed of the menu's expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.open, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); In mobileNav.close, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion toggle does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The FAQ accordion open/close functions do not update aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users are not notified of the answer's expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open, add: item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true'); In faqAccordion.close, add: item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggle does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle (grid/list) does not update aria-pressed on the toggle buttons. Screen reader users cannot tell which view is active, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _onViewToggle, add: qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => b.setAttribute('aria-pressed', b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === mode ? 'true' : 'false'));

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop does not announce to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The scroll-to-top button does not announce the action to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may not know the page scrolled to top, degrading experience.
issuesFound.fix: In _onClick, add: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');

issuesFound.title: productFilter does not announce filter changes
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the updated product count is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users are not informed of the dynamic content change, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`Now showing ${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`);

issuesFound.title: pricing does not announce billing period change
issuesFound.explanation: Changing the billing period updates prices but does not announce the change to screen readers. Users with visual impairments may miss the update, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: In setPeriod, add: liveRegion.announce(`Switched to ${period} billing. ${period === 'year' ? 'Save 20%' : ''}`);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded initialized on page load. Screen reader users may not know the initial state of the menu, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init, after setting _trigger, add: if (_trigger) _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu does not initialize aria-expanded on page load
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons do not have aria-expanded initialized on page load. Screen reader users are not informed of the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In dropdownMenu.init, after registering each trigger, add: trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger element
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions change the visible state of the drawer but do not set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's expanded/collapsed state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2005: In mobileNav._open, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');
Line 2020: In mobileNav._close, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle switch (role="switch") changes state but does not update aria-checked. Screen reader users cannot perceive the current state (month/year) of the switch, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2520: In pricing._syncToggleState, add: setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', period === 'year' ? 'true' : 'false');

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and product cards are shown/hidden, no ARIA live region announcement is made. Screen reader users are not informed that the product grid content has changed, violating WCAG 4.1.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1540: In productFilter.applyFilters, after _updateCountBadge, add: liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${totalMatches} products`);

issuesFound.title: Form validation error not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field validation fails, the function adds CSS classes but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the input element. Screen readers won't announce the invalid state, breaking WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2820: In inlineValidator._renderResult, in the invalid branch, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');
Line 2830: In the valid branch, add: setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button does not have aria-expanded="false" set when the page loads (while the drawer is closed). Screen readers may not know the initial collapsed state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2005: In mobileNav.init, after getting _trigger, add: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: Pricing toggle switch does not update aria-checked
issuesFound.explanation: The _syncToggleState function updates CSS classes but does not set aria-checked on the toggle element with role="switch". Screen reader users are not informed of the switch state (monthly vs. annual), violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _syncToggleState, add `setAttr(_toggleEl, 'aria-checked', isYear ? 'true' : 'false');` after `toggleClass(_toggleTrack, CSS.checked, isYear);`.

issuesFound.title: Product filter changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The applyFilters function updates the product count and grid but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the number of visible products changes, degrading the user experience.
issuesFound.fix: After `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);` in applyFilters, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.announce.filterApplied ? STRINGS.announce.filterApplied(totalMatches) : `${totalMatches} products found`);`.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderResult function in inlineValidator sets CSS classes and error messages but does not set aria-invalid on invalid fields. Screen readers are not alerted to the invalid state, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In _renderResult, when result.valid is false, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`. When result.valid is true, add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation trigger does not have aria-expanded initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav init function does not set an initial aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger button. Screen readers are not informed of the initial collapsed state, causing confusion.
issuesFound.fix: In the init function, after `_trigger = qs(SELECTORS.navToggle);`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers do not have aria-expanded initialized on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The register function in dropdownMenu sets aria-haspopup and aria-controls but does not set an initial aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers are not informed of the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: In the register function, after setting aria-controls, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

issuesFound.title: Testimonials carousel slide changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The goTo function in testimonialsCarousel changes the visible slide but does not announce the change via a live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the carousel advances, degrading the user experience.
issuesFound.fix: In goTo, after `_current = target;`, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.carouselSlideAnnounce ? STRINGS.carouselSlideAnnounce.replace('{n}', target + 1).replace('{total}', _slides.length) : `Slide ${target + 1} of ${_slides.length}`);`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu toggle functions do not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions in **dropdownMenu** change panel visibility but never set or update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the trigger button, so screen‑reader users cannot know whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
In `_openInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and in `_closeInstance` add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu triggers missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: In **dropdownMenu.register** the trigger button receives `aria-haspopup` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, leaving its collapsed state undisclosed on page load.
issuesFound.fix:
After setting other ARIA attributes in `register`, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The `.nav-toggle` button toggles the mobile navigation drawer via `mobileNav.toggle()` but never updates `aria-pressed`, so assistive technologies cannot convey its on/off state.
issuesFound.fix:
In `toggle()` (or after calling `open()`/`close()`), add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(_open));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Mobile‑nav toggle button missing initial aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: During **mobileNav.init()** the trigger element is not given an initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not announced to AT.
issuesFound.fix:
In `init()` after obtaining `_trigger`, add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period buttons do not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: In **pricing._syncToggleState** the period buttons receive an active CSS class but never set `aria-pressed`, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the selected billing period.
issuesFound.fix:
Inside the loop that toggles the active class, also call `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));`.

---

issuesFound.title: Billing‑period change not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: `pricing.setPeriod()` updates UI (prices, labels) but does not provide an ARIA live announcement, so users of assistive technology are not informed that the billing period changed.
issuesFound.fix:
After the UI updates, add `liveRegion.announce(\`Billing period switched to ${BILLING_PERIODS[period].label}\`);`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: `inlineValidator.validate()` marks fields with CSS error classes but never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on invalid inputs, so AT cannot detect the error state.
issuesFound.fix:
When `result.valid` is false, call `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`.

---

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not clear aria‑invalid when field becomes valid
issuesFound.explanation: After a field passes validation, the `aria-invalid` attribute remains, causing a persistent error indication for AT users.
issuesFound.fix:
When `result.valid` is true, call `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion open function does not set aria‑expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: In **faqAccordion.open()** the trigger’s `aria-expanded` attribute is never set to `"true"` when an item is expanded, so screen readers think the item stays collapsed.
issuesFound.fix:
Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open()` function.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ triggers missing initial aria‑expanded state
issuesFound.explanation: During **faqRegistry.parse()** each trigger receives `aria-controls` but no initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute, so its collapsed state is not conveyed on load.
issuesFound.fix:
After creating the trigger, add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`.

---

issuesFound.title: FAQ accordion state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a FAQ item updates the UI but does not send an ARIA live announcement, leaving AT users unaware of the change.
issuesFound.fix: After toggling, call `liveRegion.announce(\`\${item.question} \${item.open ? 'expanded' : 'collapsed'}\`);`.

issuesFound.title: Touch target size too small for small button variant

issuesFound.title: SUPPORTS object missing 'dom' property breaks focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: The SUPPORTS object defines many feature checks but omits 'dom'. createFocusTrap.activate() checks if (SUPPORTS.dom) before attaching keyboard listeners. Since this property is undefined, the focus trap never activates, allowing keyboard focus to escape modals and drawers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 335: dom:                   typeof document !== 'undefined',
